title: pouteria pearsoniorum
intro: Pouteria pearsoniorum, also known as the Guatemalan walnut or Guatemalan tree, is a species of tree in the family Sapotaceae that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The medicinal properties of Pouteria pearsoniorum lie in its seeds, leaves, and bark, which have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Pouteria pearsoniorum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
